POL-571.title: IMPLEMENT: Seal local CodeRabbit QC to the exact PR head commit and close the artifact-timing gap
POL-571.body:
## Objective

Close the structural gap that lets GitHub CodeRabbit find actionable fixes after Polaris reports a successful local CodeRabbit QC pass, by reordering finalize's artifact commit relative to the final QC pass, sealing the reviewed SHA, gating PR creation on it, and making the convergence outcome explicit and auditable.

## Context

ANALYZE issue POL-570 traced PR #175 / cluster POL-562 (six local CodeRabbit artifacts, two repair rounds, trusted "pass" outcome) followed by GitHub CodeRabbit selecting 91 files — many tracked `.polaris` runtime artifacts — and still finding actionable fixes. Repo-analysis inspection (see `smartdocs/raw/analysis/pol-570-coderabbit-qc-convergence-analysis.md`) found the local QC pass runs, then finalize's Step 6 commit stages durable `.polaris` artifacts, then the PR is opened from that commit — so the PR head was never reviewed by local QC. Additionally, no reviewed SHA is ever captured, so the finalize trust gate cannot detect the drift even in principle, and "QC pass" is a severity-threshold concept, not a zero-findings guarantee, which is currently undocumented in finalize evidence.

## Goal

The commit that becomes the PR head is the exact commit the final local CodeRabbit pass reviewed. Any reviewable tracked change after that pass invalidates QC and forces another bounded pass or an explicit escalation — never a silent PR creation. Finalize evidence records the sealed SHA, PR head SHA, review count, and convergence outcome.

## Scope

* src/finalize/index.ts
* src/finalize/steps/06-commit.ts
* src/finalize/steps/07-push.ts
* src/finalize/steps/08-create-pr.ts
* src/finalize/artifact-policy.ts
* src/qc/repair-loop.ts
* src/qc/orchestration.ts
* src/qc/policy.ts
* src/qc/providers/coderabbit.ts
* src/qc/types.ts
* src/qc/schemas.ts

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All 6 child issues are Done.
- [ ] Durable `.polaris` artifact staging occurs before the final local QC pass, not after.
- [ ] The final local QC pass's reviewed head SHA is captured and sealed.
- [ ] PR creation is gated on the PR's intended head SHA equalling the sealed SHA; a mismatch forces another bounded QC pass or explicit escalation, never silent PR creation.
- [ ] The repair loop has an explicit, bounded multi-pass convergence contract (max rounds, stop conditions, escalation).
- [ ] Finalize evidence includes sealed reviewed SHA, PR head SHA, review count, unresolved advisory findings, and convergence outcome.
- [ ] A decision is made and implemented on `.polaris` tracked-artifact QC scope parity between local and GitHub CodeRabbit.
- [ ] A regression fixture proves sealed-SHA invalidation and bounded convergence end-to-end.
- [ ] npm run build and npm test pass.

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test

## Ordering

Cluster is internally ordered — child 1 (artifact reorder) should land before child 2 (seal + gate); child 4 (evidence) depends on child 2; child 6 (regression fixture) depends on children 1, 2, and 3.

## Non-goals

* Do not require unlimited review rounds.
* Do not disable GitHub CodeRabbit to hide the mismatch.
* Do not assume every advisory-severity suggestion must block delivery.
* Do not redesign QC provider architecture, severity taxonomy, or repair-loop dispatch beyond what's needed for bounded convergence.

POL-572.title: Reorder finalize so durable .polaris artifact staging happens before the final QC pass
POL-572.body:
## Objective

Move durable `.polaris` artifact staging so it happens before the final local CodeRabbit QC pass, so the diff QC reviews is the same diff that becomes the PR.

## Context

Part of IMPLEMENT parent POL-571 (from ANALYZE POL-570). Today, `runCompletedClusterQcWithRepair()` (`src/finalize/index.ts:971-986`) runs, the trusted-outcome gate passes (`finalize/index.ts:316-401,988-999`), and only then does `06-commit.ts` stage durable artifacts via `getArtifactPromotionStageTargets()` (`src/finalize/artifact-policy.ts:19-124`) before push and PR creation. GitHub CodeRabbit then reviews those artifacts for the first time on the PR.

## Goal

Artifact staging (today's Step 6 logic) runs before the final QC pass in the finalize sequence, so local CodeRabbit's reviewed diff includes everything that ends up in the PR diff (modulo any changes made in later steps, which subsequent children address).

## Scope

* src/finalize/index.ts
* src/finalize/steps/06-commit.ts
* src/finalize/artifact-policy.ts
* src/finalize/index.test.ts

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Durable artifact staging occurs before `runCompletedClusterQcWithRepair()` runs, not after.
- [ ] The final QC pass's reviewed diff includes the staged artifact changes.
- [ ] Existing finalize step ordering tests updated to reflect the new sequence.
- [ ] No behavior change to which artifacts are staged — only the ordering relative to QC.
- [ ] npm run build and npm test pass.

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test
* npx vitest run src/finalize/index.test.ts

## Ordering

None — should land first; child issues sealing the reviewed SHA and building the regression fixture depend on this reordering.

## Non-goals

* Do not change which paths `getArtifactPromotionStageTargets()` selects.
* Do not change the QC provider or repair-loop internals.

POL-573.title: Capture and seal the local-QC-reviewed head SHA; gate PR creation on head match
POL-573.body:
## Objective

Record which commit SHA the final local CodeRabbit QC pass actually reviewed, seal it, and block PR creation unless the PR's intended head equals that sealed SHA.

## Context

Part of IMPLEMENT parent POL-571 (from ANALYZE POL-570). `QcResult` (`src/qc/types.ts:124-142`) has no `headSha`/`reviewedSha` field — only a per-finding `commitSha` used for attribution (`src/qc/attribution.ts:164-253`). The CodeRabbit provider invocation threads only a `baseRef`/`branch` string (`src/qc/providers/coderabbit.ts:655-671`, `src/qc/orchestration.ts:44,64,167`), never a resolved SHA. `validateQcRepairLoopGate()` (`src/finalize/index.ts:316-401`) only checks the `terminal_outcome` label, with no SHA comparison. Depends on POL-572 landing first so the reviewed diff already includes staged artifacts.

## Goal

After the final trusted QC pass, finalize captures `git rev-parse HEAD` as the sealed reviewed SHA and persists it in run state. Before PR creation, finalize verifies the PR's intended head SHA equals the sealed SHA; a mismatch blocks PR creation and requires another QC pass (bounded by existing `maxRounds`) or explicit operator escalation.

## Scope

* src/qc/types.ts
* src/qc/schemas.ts
* src/qc/orchestration.ts
* src/finalize/index.ts
* src/finalize/steps/08-create-pr.ts
* src/finalize/index.test.ts

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `QcResult` (or run state) carries the reviewed head SHA captured at the final trusted QC pass.
- [ ] The reviewed SHA is sealed into run state after `validateQcRepairLoopGate()` passes.
- [ ] PR creation is blocked if the PR's intended head SHA does not equal the sealed SHA.
- [ ] A mismatch triggers another bounded QC pass or an explicit escalation path — never a silent PR creation.
- [ ] npm run build and npm test pass.

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test
* npx vitest run src/qc/orchestration.test.ts src/finalize/index.test.ts

## Ordering

Depends on POL-572 (artifact reorder) landing first.

## Non-goals

* Do not change the repair loop's round-limit or severity-threshold semantics.
* Do not change which QC provider is invoked.

POL-574.title: Define and enforce a bounded multi-pass QC convergence policy in the repair loop
POL-574.body:
## Objective

Make the repair loop's multi-pass convergence contract explicit: maximum rounds, stop conditions, and escalation behavior when rounds are exhausted with findings still open.

## Context

Part of IMPLEMENT parent POL-571 (from ANALYZE POL-570). `src/qc/repair-loop.ts` already bounds rounds via `DEFAULT_MAX_REPAIR_ROUNDS = 2` (lines 44, 520, 562) and exits with `terminal_outcome` values `"pass"`, `"qc-disabled"`, `"no-repairable"`, `"medic-referral"`, `"operator-review"`, `"all-providers-failed"`, or `"max-rounds"` (line 929). This behavior is real but implicit — there is no documented contract for when `"max-rounds"` should escalate versus silently leave findings open, and no explicit statement of what "converged" means distinct from "trusted enough to proceed."

## Goal

The repair loop's convergence contract is explicit in code and docs: named max-round limit, the full set of terminal outcomes, which outcomes are "converged" versus "escalation required," and what happens on "max-rounds" (must escalate, never silently proceed as if converged).

## Scope

* src/qc/repair-loop.ts
* src/qc/repair-loop.test.ts
* src/qc/POLARIS.md

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] The repair loop's terminal outcomes are documented in `src/qc/POLARIS.md` with an explicit converged-vs-escalation classification.
- [ ] `"max-rounds"` termination is confirmed to never be treated as a converged/trusted outcome (already true per `TRUSTED_QC_REPAIR_OUTCOMES`; add a regression test asserting this).
- [ ] Any gap found where `"max-rounds"` could reach finalize without escalation is closed.
- [ ] npm run build and npm test pass.

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test
* npx vitest run src/qc/repair-loop.test.ts

## Ordering

None — independent of POL-572/POL-573; can run in parallel. POL-577 (regression fixture) depends on this landing.

## Non-goals

* Do not change `DEFAULT_MAX_REPAIR_ROUNDS` or the severity threshold used to decide blocking findings.
* Do not change which QC provider is invoked or how findings are routed.

POL-575.title: Extend finalize evidence with sealed SHA, PR head SHA, review count, and convergence outcome
POL-575.body:
## Objective

Make "successful local QC" an auditable, explicit statement in finalize evidence rather than an ambiguous pass/fail label.

## Context

Part of IMPLEMENT parent POL-571 (from ANALYZE POL-570). `decideQcAction()` / `computeQcPolicyDecision()` (`src/qc/policy.ts:33-61,90-128`) define "pass" as no unrouted finding at/above the `block` severity threshold — not zero findings. Depends on POL-573 (sealed reviewed SHA) landing first. Today finalize's run report (`src/finalize/run-report.ts`) has no field surfacing the sealed SHA, PR head SHA, review count, or unresolved advisory-severity findings, so operators reading "QC passed" cannot tell whether it means zero findings, zero blocking findings, or true convergence.

## Goal

Finalize's generated evidence (run report / Linear comment / PR description, whichever is the existing evidence surface) includes: sealed reviewed SHA, PR head SHA, number of QC review passes run, count of unresolved advisory-severity (non-blocking) findings, and the repair loop's convergence/terminal outcome.

## Scope

* src/finalize/run-report.ts
* src/finalize/run-report.test.ts
* src/finalize/index.ts
* src/finalize/linear.ts

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Finalize evidence includes sealed reviewed SHA and PR head SHA.
- [ ] Finalize evidence includes QC review pass count and unresolved advisory-severity finding count.
- [ ] Finalize evidence includes the repair loop's terminal/convergence outcome.
- [ ] Existing run-report tests updated/extended to assert the new fields are populated.
- [ ] npm run build and npm test pass.

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test
* npx vitest run src/finalize/run-report.test.ts

## Ordering

Depends on POL-573 (sealed SHA) landing first.

## Non-goals

* Do not change how findings are classified as blocking vs advisory.
* Do not add a new external reporting destination beyond finalize's existing evidence surfaces.

POL-576.title: Decide and implement .polaris tracked-artifact QC scope parity between local and GitHub CodeRabbit
POL-576.body:
## Objective

Decide, document, and implement one explicit policy for whether tracked `.polaris/` runtime artifacts should be in scope for CodeRabbit review (local and/or GitHub App), instead of the current implicit state where no exclusion configuration exists anywhere.

## Context

Part of IMPLEMENT parent POL-571 (from ANALYZE POL-570). Repo-analysis confirmed there is no root `.coderabbit.yaml`/`.coderabbit.yml`, and `QcProviderConfig` has no path-filter/exclusion field for local QC (`src/qc/providers/coderabbit.ts:648-661`) — so the 91-file GitHub scope on PR #175 included many tracked `.polaris` artifacts with no counter-evidence of an intentional split. Once POL-572 lands, local QC will review those artifacts too, but this issue is the explicit decision-and-implementation step for whether that's the right final state, or whether some `.polaris` paths should be excluded from CodeRabbit review scope entirely (locally and/or on GitHub via `.coderabbit.yaml`).

## Goal

A documented decision (in `src/qc/POLARIS.md` or a doctrine doc) states which `.polaris/` paths are legitimate durable evidence subject to review versus noise that should be excluded from CodeRabbit scope, and that decision is implemented consistently for both local QC and the GitHub App (via `.coderabbit.yaml` if exclusion is chosen).

## Scope

* .coderabbit.yaml
* src/qc/POLARIS.md
* src/finalize/artifact-policy.ts
* src/qc/providers/coderabbit.ts

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] A documented decision exists on which `.polaris/` paths are in/out of CodeRabbit review scope.
- [ ] The decision is implemented consistently for local QC and the GitHub App (e.g. `.coderabbit.yaml` exclusions, or explicit confirmation that all durable artifacts are legitimately reviewable).
- [ ] No mismatch remains between what local QC reviews and what GitHub CodeRabbit reviews for `.polaris/` paths.
- [ ] npm run build and npm test pass.

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test

## Ordering

Independent — can run in parallel with other children, but should be read alongside POL-572's outcome since that changes what local QC already reviews.

## Non-goals

* Do not change the artifact promotion logic's selection of which paths are durable (`getArtifactPromotionStageTargets()`) — only CodeRabbit's review scope.
* Do not exclude all `.polaris` files by default without an explicit documented rationale.

POL-577.title: Add regression fixture proving sealed-SHA invalidation and bounded QC convergence
POL-577.body:
## Objective

Prove end-to-end, with a controlled fixture, that the reordered artifact staging, sealed-SHA gate, and bounded convergence policy actually close the gap POL-570 identified.

## Context

Part of IMPLEMENT parent POL-571 (from ANALYZE POL-570). Depends on POL-572 (artifact reorder), POL-573 (sealed SHA gate), and POL-574 (bounded convergence contract) landing first, since this fixture exercises all three together. POL-570's own Validation section specifies the required test shape.

## Goal

A fixture-driven regression test plants a finding, repairs it while introducing a second defect, verifies another review catches it, continues until the defined convergence condition is reached, then mutates a reviewable file after the sealed pass and verifies finalize invalidates QC and blocks PR creation unless the PR head equals the sealed reviewed SHA.

## Scope

* src/finalize/index.test.ts
* src/qc/repair-loop.test.ts
* src/qc/fixtures/

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] A fixture simulates: planted finding → repair introduces a second defect → second review catches it → convergence reached.
- [ ] A test mutates a reviewable tracked file after the sealed pass and asserts finalize invalidates QC (does not proceed to PR creation with the stale seal).
- [ ] A test asserts PR creation is only allowed when PR head SHA equals the sealed reviewed SHA.
- [ ] npm run build and npm test pass.

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test
* npx vitest run src/finalize/index.test.ts src/qc/repair-loop.test.ts

## Ordering

Depends on POL-572, POL-573, and POL-574 landing first.

## Non-goals

* Do not add a live end-to-end test that calls the real CodeRabbit CLI or GitHub API — use fixtures/mocks consistent with existing QC test patterns.

POL-570.title: ANALYZE: Prove local CodeRabbit QC converges on the final PR commit
